probe.observations: ROUND 6 ENVIRONMENT: rock-solid. __vox.errors stayed empty across two full sessions of heavy stepping (~250s simulated, dozens of teleports, UI interactions); console shows only the pre-existing three.js r160 deprecation warning. HUD FPS read 60 when sampled post-reload (the headless 0 reading from earlier rounds appeared once then recovered — still do not dock perf). HUD text line remains throttled/stale in headless screenshots (showed old XYZ/biome after teleports). GAME FEEL: the smelting loop is the real thing — furnace panel is genuinely polished (live 'burn 37s · 62%' readout, take-only output slot with orange border, tools render durability bars, the whole flow works through actual UI mouse events: output-slot pickup onto a cursor stack, deposit into inventory, MC-style). Weather is the other star: a pure deterministic function of game time (bit-identical 61-sample schedule across two loads), it starts and stops BY ITSELF (rain began unprompted at gt~120 exactly where the schedule said), couples to biome/altitude (rain in plains/forest, snow at columns h>=45 or snowy biome, NOTHING in desert), dims the sky (#73adfc -> #6f94c6 at noon), tags the HUD, and logs a 'rain' sfx event with a real audio bed generator. THE VIEWMODEL DISPUTE IS SETTLED IN THE JUDGES' FAVOR: there is no held-item viewmodel — camera.children is empty, the camera isn't even parented into the scene, the file contains exactly one renderer.render(scene,camera), zero camera.add, zero 'held/viewmodel' strings, and a screenshot with a torch selected and UI hidden shows an empty bottom-right; round-5's 'orange head bottom-right' was almost certainly a placed world torch. The camera-tracking Group I found in the scene is the sky (sun/moon/stars). QUIRKS for judges: (1) calling __vox.look() with no args poisons yaw/pitch to NaN until the next setCam (harness-only; cost me two evals); (2) precipitation kind is sampled at the player's column, so particles render over the desert horizon when you stand at a border (cosmetic); (3) setTime() rewrites gt so it also time-travels the weather schedule — coherent but surprising; (4) builder-flagged: snow particles are pure white at night. Incidental finds: pick durability is real (stone pick 132, -1 per break; iron pick 251); reach limit enforced at exactly ~5 blocks (ray returns null at 5.05 — caused one amusing failed mine); controls overlay already documents the new mechanics ('Shift sneak slow, edge-safe', 'L-click hold to mine, hardness + tools matter'). Screenshot inventory: furnace UI mid-burn, rain over forest/ocean aerial, ground-level natural rain over plains with flowers + desert border, snowfall flakes at the snow line, torch-selected empty bottom-right (viewmodel disproof), controls overlay. The crafting->mining->smelting->tools->food chain plus self-running weather makes this round feel like an actual survival game, not a tech demo.

panel.summary: Round 6 is the cleanest worldgen-seat delta of the run: weather landed as a genuinely worldgen-coupled system (a pure deterministic function of gt with bit-identical schedules across loads, natural onset exactly on schedule, and rain/snow/none keyed to biome and the verified h>=45 snow line), while two long-owed checklist items finally converted on hard scans - depth-ore rarity (1468 iron all at y2-14 vs 1731 coal at y9-27) and coherent biome regions (12 plains-buffered regions over two 1200-block transects, zero confetti). Round 5's only worldgen-domain fail is verifiably fixed (NaN/Infinity spawns rejected, vertical escapes culled at y<-16), and the smelting family closes the loop my ore stratification feeds: deep iron now mines, smelts, and gates the iron pick. On the dispute, I rule with the round-5 dissenters: there is no held-item viewmodel - the scene-graph audit (empty camera.children, one renderer.render, sky as the only camera-tracking group) plus the torch-selected empty-bottom-right screenshot settle it as a fail, and the round-5 sighting was a placed world torch. What still caps my domain is the advertised-absent residue: rivers and overhangs are six-round no-shows the header still claims, cave mouths and foliage tint remain unsighted, villages hold at a third of their weight, and the light engine is occlusion-free falloff. I move 40 to 41.5: two weight-2 families converted whole plus ~2 itemized conversions and a cleared fail, with the half-point held back (ties downward) for the unwitnessed header claims and the new look() harness wart.

Residual swing ~±1 MPS: the carried memory-growth gaps (unbounded chunks Map, CCmap cliff-clear, autosave swallow) bite only on sessions far longer than any probe has run, and the panel could price the weather/smelting families anywhere from 3 to 4 of their combined 4 weights.

panel.summary: Round 6 through the perf/stability lens is a clean sweep of my round-5 ledger: both verified fails are fixed in source and re-verified in play (non-finite spawn rejection plus the y<-16 vertical cull at index.html:783-850, and the simNow sim clock at 623/1169 that closed the mining/place/eat clock seam, converting hold-to-break and tool-speed timing into deterministic evidence), and my predecessor's frame-guard gap landed too — the loop is now split into sim and render guards that both surface into __vox.errors. The two new families arrived with the same engineering discipline the round-5 panel praised: weather is a pure function of gt with a pooled, allocation-free 600-particle system (bit-identical schedule across loads), and smelting is a placement-bounded Map with delete-on-break spill and step()-coherent burn timing, all verified through real UI mouse events. Stability is the best of the run — ~250s of stepped sim across two sessions with __vox.errors empty — and I rule the viewmodel dispute against the wow item: no camera-attached mesh exists, one renderer.render, empty bottom-right with a torch selected; round-5's sighting was a placed world torch. The domain's only open fails are the carried memory debts (unbounded chunks Map now in its 5th round, the CCmap cliff-clear that weather's 600-per-frame CI lookups make more dangerous, and the quota-swallowing autosave) plus the new harness-only look() NaN wart, none of which bite at probe timescales. I land at 42, +2 over the round-5 merge, and the delta is conversion-driven: four fully live-verified systems-family weights (smelting 2, weather 2) plus two fixed fails, with no advance credit anywhere in it.

lanes.name: Lane 1 — Probe-filed fixes: harness guards, real held-item viewmodel, weather visual polish + perf hook
lanes.goal: Clear both probe-filed fails from this round (the __vox.look() NaN poisoning and the now-settled-absent held-item viewmodel), retire the two weather cosmetics three judges flagged as fails-in-waiting against the freshly credited weight-2 family, and add the __vox.perf instrumentation that converts three carried perf unknowns. ~0.7-0.8 MPS, low risk, all isolated from the mesher/worldgen/sim hot paths.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. (1) GUARD applyLook (line 620): first statement `if(!isFinite(mx)||!isFinite(my))return;` — clears the filed fail where __vox.look() with no args runs applyLook(undefined,undefined) and NaN-poisons yaw/pitch until the next setCam. Also guard __vox.setCam (line 1194): early-return false if any of x,y,z,ya,pi is non-finite, leaving all state untouched; return true on success. Do NOT alter happy-path math. (2) HELD-ITEM VIEWMODEL: add `scene.add(camera)` immediately after camera creation (~line 387) — three.js only renders camera children when the camera is in the scene graph; this is the missing prerequisite the scene-graph audit exposed. Create `const vmMat=new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({map:atlas,alphaTest:.4,depthTest:false});` near matDrop (line 731). Module state `let vmMesh=null,vmId=-1,vmSwing=0;`. Function updVM(dt): read `const it=inv[slot],id=it?it.id:0;` if id!==vmId, remove old mesh from camera and, if id>0, build `new THREE.Mesh(dropGeo(id),vmMat)` (dropGeo at line 733 already handles block tiles, BL.cr cross-tiles like torch 39, and ITILE item tiles), set renderOrder=999, scale ~2.2 (dropGeo is .25-scale, net ~0.55 blocks), base position ≈ (0.42,-0.34,-0.62) in camera space with rotation.y≈Math.PI/5, then camera.add(vmMesh). Each frame: vmMat.color.copy(matO.color) so it tracks day/night/storm light; swing: when mL is true or within 0.25s of an attack/break set vmSwing=1, decay vmSwing-=dt*4 clamped at 0, and offset position.y/rotation.x by a sin dip proportional to vmSwing. Call updVM(dt) from loop() guard B after updHL() (line 1186) — render-side only, NEVER inside simStep, so determinism and the pause-freeze envelope are untouched. __vox.hideUI must not affect it (it is scene-side; verify). Add harness helper `vm:()=>({id:vmId,children:camera.children.length})` to __vox. (3) WEATHER POLISH, contained to updWeather/wxSpawn (lines 956-973): (a) night snow — replace `wxMat.color.setRGB(1,1,1)` at line 967 with a copy of matO.color (flakes inherit the day/night/storm tint; unchanged at noon since matO≈white); (b) desert-border bleed — in wxSpawn (line 956), after picking p.x/p.z, re-roll the position up to 4 times if `CI(Math.floor(p.x),Math.floor(p.z)).biome==='desert'` while precipitation is active (bounded retries; wxSpawn already does one CI call so cost is trivial). Do NOT touch weatherAt (line 950) or the wKind decision (line 961) — the deterministic schedule is the verified contract; particles carry no determinism contract. (4) __vox.perf HOOK: module-level `const perfFrames=new Float32Array(120);let perfFi=0;` and `const perfBuild={n:0,total:0,max:0};` In loop() guard A after dt is computed (line 1173): `perfFrames[perfFi++%120]=dt*1000;` In buildChunk (line 450): t0=performance.now() at entry, accumulate elapsed ms into perfBuild before returning. Expose `perf:()=>({frame:{avg,max over the ring},build:{n:perfBuild.n,avg:perfBuild.total/perfBuild.n||0,max:perfBuild.max}})` on __vox. Allocation-free per frame. FORBIDDEN: any edit to genChunk/CI/rawH/cave, RECIPES/attackMob/MOBDEF, furnaces, or the mesher face loops.
lanes.acceptance: Through __vox on ?test=1 (seed 1337): (a) call look() with no args, then getState() — yaw/pitch finite and unchanged, ray() still returns hits, no NaN anywhere; setCam(NaN,0,0,0,0) returns false and getState() is unchanged. (b) give(22,1) + select that slot: vm() reports {id:22,children:>=1}; hideUI() + screenshot shows the held torch bottom-right (the exact shot that proved absence this round now shows presence); switch to an empty slot — vm().id===0 and the mesh is gone; select stone — mesh swaps; mineHold(true)+step(1) then screenshot mid-swing shows the dip. lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. (2) LAYOUT — entire footprint inside local 1..14 so it is single-chunk (no -3..18 overlap coherence needed): floor level F=mx; under each built cell, fill from the column's h+1 up to F with dirt (2) via tset(...,1). Hut A at local (3..7)x(3..7): plank (16) walls F+1..F+3 with log (6) corners, a 1x2 doorway gap mid-wall facing the path, interior force-cleared to air, flat plank roof at F+4; interior crafting table (17) against a back wall. Hut B at local (9..13)x(8..12): same shell, interior furnace (23). Dirt (2) path replacing the surface block at F between the two doorways. Two torch posts along the path, each a log at F+1..F+2 with a torch (22) at F+3, positioned within local 5..10 so the 7-block light pools barely cross chunk borders (known faint edge: a neighbor meshed before this chunk gens won't show pool spill until its next remesh — acceptable, document in a code comment). (3) TORCH REGISTRY SYNC — gen-placed torches currently never light or suppress spawns because torchR (line 371) is setB-only and buildChunk's tlist (lines 456-457) plus lightAtW (line 820) read torchR. AFTER the editB replay (line 364), if hamletPlaced: scan the chunk's d[] for remaining id-22 cells and register each into torchR keyed exactly like setB does (Map 'cx,cz' -> Map(cellIndex -> [wx,wy,wz])). built.lane: Lane 1 — Probe-filed fixes: harness guards, real held-item viewmodel, weather visual polish + perf hook
built.changes_summary:
All edits in game/index.html (single file, now 1295 lines, still ends </html>).

(1) HARNESS GUARDS: applyLook (~line 622) now opens with `if(!isFinite(mx)||!isFinite(my))return;` — __vox.look() with no args no longer NaN-poisons yaw/pitch. __vox.setCam (~line 1216) early-returns false if any of x/y/z/ya/pi is non-finite (state untouched) and returns true on success; happy-path math untouched.

(2) HELD-ITEM VIEWMODEL: `scene.add(camera)` added right after camera creation (~line 388) — the missing scene-graph prerequisite. New block after dropGeo (~lines 752-766): vmMat (atlas, alphaTest .4, depthTest false), state vmMesh/vmId/vmSwing/vmHitT, and updVM(dt) which rebuilds the camera-child mesh from dropGeo(id) on slot/id change (renderOrder 999), copies matO.color each frame (day/night/storm tint), and applies a sin dip to position.y/rotation.x driven by vmSwing (held at 1 while mL or within 250ms of a left-click via vmHitT set in the mousedown handler at ~line 648, decays at dt*4). Called from loop() guard B after updHL() (~line 1208) — render-side only, never in simStep. Harness helper `vm:()=>({id:vmId,children:camera.children.length})` added to __vox. NOTE: tuned transform to scale 1.8 at (0.5,-0.42,-0.8) instead of the spec's literal 2.2 at (0.42,-0.34,-0.62) — the literal numbers rendered the cube across ~half the screen (verified by screenshot); spec's "~/≈" latitude used, re-verified visually as a proper bottom-right held item.

(3) WEATHER POLISH (contained to updWeather/wxSpawn): snow branch now does wxMat.color.copy(matO.color) (~line 988) so flakes inherit the night/storm palette (unchanged ~white at noon); wxSpawn (~line 976) re-rolls x/z up to 4 times while the column is desert (bounded, one cached CI call per attempt). weatherAt and the wKind decision untouched.

(4) __vox.perf: perfFrames Float32Array(120) ring + perfFi + perfBuild{n,total,max} declared before buildChunk (~line 451); ring write in loop guard A right after dt (~line 1196); buildChunk wraps entry/exit with performance.now() accumulation (single exit point); `perf:()=>{frame:{avg,max},build:{n,avg,max}}` exposed on __vox. Allocation-free per frame.

VERIFIED LIVE on the preview server (?test=1, seed 1337): (a) look()/look(NaN,NaN) leave yaw/pitch bit-identical and finite, setCam(NaN,0,0,0,0)===false with state untouched, setCam/look happy paths intact; (b) give(22)+select → vm()={id:22,children:1} and screenshot shows the held torch bottom-right; empty slot → vm()={id:0,children:0} mesh gone; grass/stone swap works; mineHold(true)+step(1) screenshot shows the dipped/tilted swing pose; viewmodel dims with night palette; (c) midnight+setWeather(1) over a snowy column shows dim (not pure-white) flakes; standing at the plains/desert border looking into desert shows no rain streaks over desert columns; (d) perf() all-finite, build.n grew 121→137 after a 300-block cold teleport, build avg 2.7ms; (e) fresh load spawns at exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) (pre-frame check), 61-sample weatherAt sweep deterministic, pause() freezes gt and cloudOx bit-identical across rendered frames, __vox.errors empty after the full session.
built.notes: Risks/follow-ups: (1) Viewmodel transform deviates from the spec's literal numbers (scale 1.8 @ (0.5,-0.42,-0.8) vs ~2.2 @ ≈(0.42,-0.34,-0.62)) — the literal values rendered a ~half-screen cube; screenshots confirm the tuned pose reads as a proper held item bottom-right. Flag to judges in case they diff against the literal spec. (2) perf() frame avg/max measured in this backgrounded preview tab are inflated (rAF throttled to screenshot-driven frames, dt gaps up to 81s land in the ring) — environmental, not a code issue; a visible/headless run will show real ~8ms frames. (3) Desert re-roll is bounded (5 attempts), so ~(desert fraction)^5 residual leakage per particle remains at a border (~3% when half the field is desert) — per spec's bounded-retries design; the acceptance shot shows a clean desert. (4) The swing kick for mob-attack clicks is wired in the mousedown handler (vmHitT), not attackMob (attackMob edits are forbidden by the lane); synthetic __vox.attack() calls therefore don't kick the swing — mineHold does, which is what the acceptance tests. (5) vmMesh shares cached dropGeo geometries with drops — intentional, so no dispose on swap.

built.notes: Risks/follow-ups: (a) torches() only lists hamlet torches once the hamlet chunk has generated; the findStructures hamlet pass deliberately gens hit chunks, so the acceptance flow (findStructures(768) then probe) works on a fresh load, as does teleporting there. (d) Documented faint edge in code: a neighbor chunk meshed before the hamlet chunk gens won't show torch-pool spill across the border until its next remesh. (e) spawnAt on dirt-filled (sub-F) hamlet columns returns 'solid' since CI.h+1 sits inside the fill — torch-lit columns at natural h==F (path cells, footprint-adjacent) return 'light' as required; prober should pick such a column. (f) Hut interiors render sky-bright because skylight uses the CI heightmap (roofs invisible to it) — pre-existing behavior for all built structures, not lane scope.
